title: Catalan Opening: Closed Variation
summary: Learn the strategic Catalan, where White plays a reversed Benoni for a small but lasting advantage.
description: The Catalan is one of White's most prestigious openings. By playing d4 and then g3/Bg2, White creates a reversed Benoni structure with a slight edge. This tutorial covers the main line, typical plans, and endgame techniques.
line: 1.d4 d5 2.c4 e6 3.Nf3 Nf6 4.g3 Be7 5.Bg2 O-O 6.O-O dxc4

variations.decline: 6...c6 - The Catalan Declined, avoiding the exchange

objectives: Understand the Catalan structure: why it's positionally desirable
Learn why the bishop pair and central control matter
Master typical plans: e3, Nbd2, or Ne5 depending on position
plans: White's plan: Use the bishop pair and central control for a small edge
Black's plan: Exchange pieces and create counterplay on the queenside
The move ...dxc4 gives White a small but lasting advantage
White should avoid exchanging - keep pieces for the advantage
commonMistakes: Do not think the Catalan is boring - there are many subtle ideas
Avoid playing too passively - maintain the initiative
Do not underestimate Black's activity - the queen and bishop can be dangerous
White should prepare e3 carefully before pushing e4
completionTitle: Strategic position with White's slight edge
completionSummary: The Catalan is a prestigious opening. You've learned the main ideas and can navigate the position.

steps.title: Claim central space
steps.instruction: Play d4 to control the center.
steps.coaching: The Catalan is one of White's most strategic openings.
steps.hint: Push the queen pawn two squares.

steps.title: Black responds in the center
steps.instruction: Black plays d5, challenging White's center.
steps.coaching: Black meets the Catalan with a solid central reply.

steps.title: The Catalan move
steps.coaching: This is the Catalan! White offers a reversed Benoni.

steps.title: Black plays e6
steps.instruction: Black plays e6, preparing the Queen's Gambit structure.
steps.coaching: Black is setting up the Catalan structure.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nf3, developing toward the center.

steps.title: The Catalan fianchetto
steps.coaching: This is the Catalan setup! White fianchettoes the bishop.

steps.title: Black develops the bishop
steps.coaching: Black develops the bishop, preparing castling.

steps.title: The Catalan bishop
steps.instruction: Move the bishop to g2, controlling the long diagonal.
steps.coaching: This is the key move! The bishop controls e4, d5, and c6.

steps.title: Black castles
steps.coaching: Black gets the king to safety.

steps.title: Black accepts the gambit
steps.coaching: This is the Catalan Accepted. Black takes the pawn.

steps.title: Develop the queen
steps.instruction: Move the queen to c2, supporting the pawn.
steps.coaching: White develops the queen, preparing to regain the pawn.

steps.title: Black pushes b5
steps.instruction: Black pushes b5, creating queenside pressure.
steps.coaching: Black creates counterplay on the queenside.

steps.title: Challenge Black's expansion
steps.coaching: White meets the queenside play with a4.

steps.title: Exchange on b5
steps.instruction: Capture on b5 with the a-pawn.

steps.title: Black recaptures
steps.coaching: Black gets a pawn on b5 but loses castling rights.
